findings.text: upper_bound_certificate.json uses 'verified': true instead of a 'status' field. Downstream tooling expecting 'status=PROVEN_TIGHT' will not find it.
Pigeonhole argument in bnb_upper_bound.py is correctly implemented. Early-exit guard (target > row_cap * N) fires before tree traversal — branches_explored=0 at target=21.
solution.py POINTS is byte-identical to parent orbit/02-deterministic-closed-form.r1. No silent mutation; C4-invariance and no-3-collinear assertions pass.
Certificate runtime numbers match committed log.md values (1.14e-05s, 2.13s, 1.1M branches).
Panel (b) log-scale cannot represent 0 branches for target=21; bar appears at y=10^-1, label '0' clarifies but framing is misleading.
findings.fix: Linear scale, broken axis, or symlog.

findings.text: Annotation 'y=9' conflates row index with Cartesian y-coordinate.

findings.issue: 1185 words above style.md 500-word soft limit; justified by proof content but not annotated.
findings.fix: Add one-sentence length justification at the top.

findings.text: Certificate lacks top-level 'status' field. Consider adding 'status': 'PROVEN_TIGHT' for machine-readability.
No research/references/registry.yaml — references live inline in log.md. Campaign-level gap.
Prior Art & Novelty section is exemplary — clear, honest.
Significance 'Noise' reflects delta_metric=0 vs parent by design; scientific value is the proof certificate, not the metric.

synopsis: Orbit 03 is a well-executed consolidation orbit proving 20 is the tight upper bound for n=10 via textbook row-pigeonhole. B&B driver verifiably returns 0 branches at target=21; POINTS byte-identical to parent; proof mathematically sound. Two minor figure issues: log-scale can't render 0 branches; narrative.png uses 'y=9' where 'row 9' would be clearer.
